evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Uses current clerkMiddleware from @clerk/nextjs/server (not deprecated authMiddleware). ClerkProvider, SignedIn/SignedOut, UserButton, SignIn component usage consistent. Minor concern: instructs naming middleware file 'proxy.ts' which is unusual (Next.js convention is middleware.ts); also marks root layout 'use client' which conflicts with metadata patterns but is allowed.
evidence.executability: Fenced blocks parse. However, middleware file name 'proxy.ts' will NOT be picked up by Next.js — only middleware.ts at root/src is recognized. Also app/layout.tsx marked 'use client' wraps ClerkProvider which works but is non-standard. auth() destructuring of isAuthenticated is acceptable in current API. Multiple non-obvious fixes needed.
evidence.completeness: (a) API key acquisition with dashboard URL present; (b) install commands in bash; (c) env var template with exact names (NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, CLERK_SECRET_KEY); (d) server route code present; (e) frontend components present; (f) test commands (jest install + cypress described) present but no single runnable test command. Env vars listed match those referenced.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Multiple official clerk.com/docs URLs placed inline near relevant code sections (quickstart, middleware reference, social connections, env variables). Canonical domain used throughout.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable=true; long-tail handling applied — internally consistent with version claims. Significant executability concern: 'proxy.ts' naming instruction breaks Next.js middleware detection; extractor would write a non-functional middleware file.
